provost.name: Vacant; Laurie Lauzon Clabo serving as senior adviser through May 2026

ai_strategy_lead.name: Dongxiao Zhu
ai_strategy_lead.title: Co-Director, Institute for AI and Data Science (AIDaS); Founder and Inaugural Director, Wayne AI Research Initiative

documents.title: Grand Challenges: Powering innovation with AI (one of four research pillars)
Institute for AI and Data Science (AIDaS) charter, approved by Board of Governors
Ad Hoc AI Committee Running Report (Fall 2024 & Winter 2025)

ai_strategy.summary: Wayne State's AI strategy is anchored in its September 2025 Grand Challenges initiative, which identifies AI as one of four research pillars alongside health, mobility, and environmental sustainability. The Board of Governors approved the six-year charter for the Institute for AI and Data Science (AIDaS) on October 23, 2025, creating an interdisciplinary hub reporting to the Vice President for Research & Innovation. The Division of Academic Affairs designated 2025-26 as the 'AI in Action' Year of Focus, organizing a campus-wide programming series on AI's impact on education, work, community, and the environment. The strategy emphasizes 'responsible human-AI collaborations' and ethical AI methodologies, with Wayne State positioning itself as Michigan's only urban R1 institution in AI research.

key_provisions: All AI tools must be reviewed and approved by Computing & Information Technology (C&IT) before use
Third-party unvetted AI tools are not approved; users must select from C&IT-approved list
Prohibits input of FERPA-protected student information, PII/PHI, enterprise reporting data (Cognos, Power BI), and cell sizes under 10 into AI systems
Research use must follow NIH and NSF policies on AI in grant proposals and peer review
Board of Governors amended Student Code of Conduct (2.31.02) to require students to follow instructor AI guidance in each course
Student Code of Conduct treats unauthorized AI use in coursework as cheating
Academic Senate AI subcommittee developed optional syllabus language templates for faculty
Warns against AI-enabled phishing, deepfakes, and unauthorized meeting bots
ai_policy.summary: Wayne State operates a cautious but comprehensive AI policy framework. The core Guidelines for Using Generative AI (tech.wayne.edu/policies/aiguidelines) require C&IT approval for any AI tool before use, with a short approved list that includes WarriorGPT, Microsoft Copilot, Google Vertex AI, Cursor, Zoom AI Companion, and (with exception approval) ChatGPT and Claude. The Board of Governors amended the Student Code of Conduct in 2025 to formalize instructor discretion: students must follow course-level AI guidance, with syllabus language templates provided by the Academic Senate's ad hoc AI subcommittee. Data protection is explicit and strict, with FERPA and PII firmly off-limits for external AI systems.

bodies.name: Institute for AI and Data Science (AIDaS)
bodies.scope: Six-year chartered interdisciplinary institute integrating Wayne AI Research Initiative, Data Science and Business Analytics, and Computation/AI/Data Science groups; reports to VP for Research & Innovation; co-directed by Dr. Dongxiao Zhu and Dr. Hengguang Li

bodies.name: Ad Hoc AI Committee (Academic Senate)
bodies.scope: Faculty governance committee chaired by Richard Pineau; developed syllabus language templates and the amendment to the Student Code of Conduct that codifies instructor AI policy authority; issues annual running reports

bodies.name: Grand Challenges AI Pillar
bodies.scope: Launched September 10, 2025 by President Kimberly Espy and VP for Research Ezemenari Obasi as one of four university-wide research pillars

bodies.name: Wayne AI Research Initiative
bodies.scope: Pre-existing research coordinating group founded by Dr. Dongxiao Zhu, now integrated into AIDaS; covers computer vision, NLP, cybersecurity, mobility, healthcare, and 'intelligent reality'

bodies.name: Year of Focus Steering (Office of the Provost)
bodies.scope: Coordinates the 2025-26 'AI in Action' Year of Focus programming across Wayne AI, Office for Teaching & Learning, and the Computation/AI/Data Science Initiative

governance_structure.summary: Governance is distributed across research, faculty senate, and provost offices. AIDaS is the flagship operational body created in October 2025, with a six-year charter and co-direction from computer science and mathematics. Faculty governance runs through the Academic Senate's Ad Hoc AI Committee (which has been active since at least Winter 2023), which authored the Student Code of Conduct amendment passed by the Board of Governors in 2025. There is no single Chief AI Officer role; strategy is effectively shared between the VP for Research & Innovation, the CIO, and the Academic Senate.

initiatives.title: Institute for AI and Data Science (AIDaS) established with six-year Board charter
initiatives.description: On October 23, 2025, Wayne State's Board of Governors approved a six-year charter establishing AIDaS as an interdisciplinary institute hosting research across engineering, medicine, public health, education, business, social sciences, and liberal arts. The institute integrates the Wayne AI Research Initiative, Data Science and Business Analytics program, and Computation/AI/Data Science groups, and is co-directed by Dr. Dongxiao Zhu (Computer Science) and Dr. Hengguang Li (Mathematics).
initiatives.significance: Signals institutional commitment to AI at Michigan's only urban R1 and aligns AI research with the broader Grand Challenges research strategy.

investment.description: Six-year chartered institute; specific funding envelope not disclosed in public announcement

partners: James and Patricia Anderson College of Engineering
College of Liberal Arts and Sciences
collaborations.description: Internal cross-college institute; intentional interdisciplinary design

initiatives.policy_implications: Reports directly to VP for Research & Innovation; provides governance home for ethical AI research methodologies

initiatives.title: Grand Challenges initiative with AI as one of four research pillars
initiatives.description: President Kimberly Espy and VP for Research & Innovation Dr. Ezemenari Obasi launched Grand Challenges on September 10, 2025, identifying four pillars: lifelong health, sustainable environments, AI innovation, and smart mobility. The AI pillar asks 'How can we leverage responsible human-AI collaborations to drive discovery, innovation and economic growth?'
initiatives.significance: Embeds AI in a top-level research strategy and connects it to three domain pillars (health, environment, mobility), driving cross-pillar funding alignment.

investment.funding_source: Wayne State University internal and anticipated external grants
investment.description: University-wide research framework; specific Grand Challenges envelope not disclosed

partners: All Wayne State colleges and research units

initiatives.policy_implications: Defines 'responsible human-AI collaboration' as institutional framing

initiatives.title: WarriorGPT — private generative AI platform for faculty, staff, and students
initiatives.description: C&IT developed WarriorGPT, a Wayne State-hosted generative AI chatbot running on open-source LLMs. It is available to faculty and staff for instructional and administrative use, and faculty can enable it in Canvas to grant student access at the course level. The platform does not retain user inputs or train on conversations, mitigating institutional data risk from external consumer AI tools.
initiatives.significance: Provides a FERPA-aware institutional alternative to consumer AI tools and is the approved default for work with non-public Wayne State data.

investment.description: Internally built platform hosted on Wayne State infrastructure; cost not disclosed

collaborations.description: Internal C&IT build using open-source LLMs

tools_and_vendors.name: Canvas LMS
tools_and_vendors.role: Delivery channel for course-level WarriorGPT access

initiatives.policy_implications: Designed explicitly to solve the data-exfiltration problem of external consumer AI services

initiatives.title: Guidelines for Using Generative AI + approved tools list
initiatives.description: C&IT publishes comprehensive generative AI guidelines that require any AI tool to be reviewed and approved before use. The approved list currently includes WarriorGPT, Cursor AI, Google Vertex AI, Microsoft Copilot, Microsoft Copilot Chat, Microsoft Teams Premium, Zoom AI Companion, ChatGPT (with C&IT exception), and Claude (with C&IT exception). Guidelines prohibit input of FERPA-protected data, PII/PHI, and other sensitive categories.
initiatives.significance: One of the more concrete and restrictive AI vendor-approval regimes in the Midwest, with published whitelist and explicit enterprise testing mentioned (Copilot, Zoom AI, Canvas AI).

collaborations.description: Enterprise vendor agreements with data protection terms

tools_and_vendors.name: Microsoft Copilot Chat
tools_and_vendors.role: Public GPT bundled with M365 license

initiatives.policy_implications: Whitelist approach; anything not on the list is not permitted without exception

initiatives.title: Board of Governors amends Student Code of Conduct for AI
initiatives.description: The Wayne State Board of Governors amended Code 2.31.02 (Student Code of Conduct) in 2025 following a recommendation from the Academic Senate's Ad Hoc AI Committee. The amendment does not ban AI; it requires students to follow course-level instructor guidance on when and how AI may be used. The AI subcommittee developed syllabus language templates for faculty. Unauthorized AI use in coursework is treated as cheating.
initiatives.significance: Provides a durable governance mechanism for faculty-level AI policy without prescribing a single institutional stance, preserving pedagogical discretion.

investment.description: Policy change with no disclosed budget impact

initiatives.policy_implications: Locks in instructor discretion as the unit of AI policy enforcement

initiatives.title: IntelliMake research hub and pilot-scale AI factory with Kyndryl
initiatives.description: On January 13, 2026, Wayne State and Kyndryl announced a multi-year collaboration to establish the IntelliMake research hub and a pilot-scale factory inside the James and Patricia Anderson College of Engineering. The hub co-develops agentic AI, robotics, and digital twin manufacturing technologies, integrated via Kyndryl Bridge. It is pitched as positioning Detroit and Michigan within a projected $10 trillion smart manufacturing market.
initiatives.significance: Rare academic-industry pilot factory for agentic AI manufacturing, anchored in Detroit's auto-industry context and explicitly workforce-oriented.

investment.description: Multi-year joint investment, amount not disclosed

collaborations.description: Multi-year co-development and workforce training collaboration

tools_and_vendors.name: Kyndryl Bridge
tools_and_vendors.role: Infrastructure orchestration for AI manufacturing demos

initiatives.title: Google Public Sector partnership and PHOENIX public health AI platform
initiatives.description: Announced June 2, 2025, Wayne State partnered with Google Public Sector to deploy Google Cloud infrastructure (BigQuery, Google Kubernetes Engine, AI Hypercomputer) across research. The flagship use case is PHOENIX (Population Health Outcomes and Information Exchange), which covers eight states from Wisconsin to Massachusetts and — with partner Syntasa — powers CHNA 2.0, cutting Community Health Needs Assessments from up to a year to weeks using Syntasa Sentiment Analytics over surveys, Google Search, and social-media data.
initiatives.significance: Concrete, operational AI-for-public-good deployment; strong evidence of applied AI research translating to measurable public-health impact in Detroit and region.

investment.funding_source: Wayne State + Google Public Sector + Syntasa
investment.description: Multi-year cloud and analytics partnership; amount not disclosed

collaborations.description: Partnership spanning research infrastructure and applied public-health AI

tools_and_vendors.name: Syntasa Sentiment Analytics
tools_and_vendors.role: Sentiment analysis over surveys, search, social media

initiatives.policy_implications: Secure, Wayne State-owned de-identified data architecture

initiatives.title: eCAT — NSF IUCRC Center for Electric, Connected and Autonomous Technologies
initiatives.description: Wayne State leads eCAT, an NSF Industry-University Cooperative Research Center (Award 2231523) launched September 15, 2023 with a five-year, $4.5 million budget ($2.25M NSF, remainder industry). Partnering with University of North Texas and Clarkson University, eCAT develops V2X communications, real-time edge computing, autonomous systems, and AI-based perception technologies. Director: Dr. Caisheng Wang; Zheng Dong is WSU site co-director.
initiatives.significance: Federal-scale research center anchoring Wayne State's AI-for-mobility agenda and linking into Detroit's automotive industry corridor.

collaborations.description: NSF-funded multi-university IUCRC with industry membership

initiatives.title: M.S. in Artificial Intelligence (three concentrations)
initiatives.description: Wayne State's James and Patricia Anderson College of Engineering offers a 30-credit M.S. in Artificial Intelligence with three concentrations: AI Hardware and Systems, AI Algorithms and Systems, and Industrial AI. The program ranked #20 nationally in the 2024 TechGuide master's rankings. A new CSC 5991 course, 'Trustworthy AI for Large Language Models and Vision-Language Models,' debuted in Winter 2026.
initiatives.significance: Dedicated AI master's with explicit industrial and hardware tracks — rare outside top-20 CS programs — and a new trustworthy-AI course placing the program in alignment with responsible AI trends.

investment.description: Ongoing graduate program in the College of Engineering

initiatives.title: AI online professional certificate and Amesite partnership
initiatives.description: Wayne State offers an online AI certificate through Knowledge on Demand / Online Programs covering markets, key players, AI technology across business/health/social media, jobs impact, and regulatory issues. In September 2020, Wayne State expanded a 2019 partnership with Amesite Inc. to offer 'stackable' AI-backed six-week certification courses covering AI, blockchain, data science, gamification, and smart manufacturing, plus mobility certifications.
initiatives.significance: Low-friction professional-development pathway for Detroit's workforce, packaged in sector-specific stackable certificates.

collaborations.description: AI-backed online learning ecosystem for Wayne State's continuing-ed certificates

initiatives.title: W the Warrior Bot — SMS conversational AI for enrollment and student services
initiatives.description: W the Warrior is a 24/7 SMS AI chatbot launched in April 2018 with AdmitHub (now Mainstay). It answers admissions and registration questions, sends deadline reminders, and escalates to staff when needed. Reported outcomes include a 14.6% increase in enrollment, 18% rise among first-generation students, and 13% rise among Pell-eligible students — particularly serving minority and underserved learners. A related service, DetroitEd411, launched in 2019 through Facebook Messenger.
initiatives.significance: Early, long-running deployment of conversational AI with documented impact on first-gen and underserved enrollment — one of the most-cited chatbot success stories in higher ed.

collaborations.description: Founding-era partnership with higher-ed chatbot startup

tools_and_vendors.name: W the Warrior Bot
tools_and_vendors.role: SMS conversational AI for student success

initiatives.title: 2025-26 Year of Focus: 'AI in Action' campus programming
initiatives.description: The Division of Academic Affairs designated the 2025-26 academic year as Year of Focus 'AI in Action,' organizing campus-wide events exploring AI's impact on education, work, community, and the environment. Confirmed programming includes an April 1, 2026 keynote with Sal Khan (Khan Academy), a March 23, 2026 panel on AI and the Future of Work, a March 25 hands-on Explore AI Together session, and an April 20 session on Community and Environmental Impacts of AI.
initiatives.significance: Embeds AI literacy into institutional culture through a year-long visible programming arc that intentionally rotates perspectives (labor, community, environment).

collaborations.description: Khan Academy's Sal Khan as keynote speaker

initiatives.title: NIH AI-enabled drug design and biomedical imaging grants
initiatives.description: Wayne State secured more than $4 million in NIH funding across multiple AI-enabled biomedical projects. Dr. Alice Walker (Chemistry) received a $1.8 million NIGMS grant to develop AI-enabled imaging tools for sugar-based drug design. Dr. Zichun Zhong received an NSF grant to build MicroVessel Processor (MVP), an AI platform for 3D microvascular imaging. Dr. Ming Dong is leading AI-generated synthetic CT imaging from MRI now in clinical trials with University of Wisconsin.
initiatives.significance: Federally-funded AI-for-biomedical research pipeline with clinical-translation pathway, reinforcing Wayne State's AI-health intersection.

investment.description: Multiple federal grants totaling over $4M for AI-enabled biomedical research

collaborations.description: Federal grant portfolio with external clinical collaborators

initiatives.title: NAOMI — NIH-funded conversational AI for obesity motivational interviewing
initiatives.description: Dr. Alexander Kotov (Computer Science) leads NAOMI (Neural Agent for Obesity Motivational Interviewing), an NIH-funded conversational AI project delivering personalized weight-loss motivation when clinicians are unavailable. It represents one of Wayne State's applied AI-for-health deployments inside a community with elevated chronic-disease burden.
initiatives.significance: Community-level applied AI in behavioral health targeting a population served directly by Wayne State's School of Medicine and Detroit community.

initiatives.title: NSF-funded LLM unlearning research (Zhu lab)
initiatives.description: Dr. Dongxiao Zhu (Computer Science, AIDaS co-director) leads NSF-funded research on large language model 'unlearning' — teaching AI systems to selectively forget toxic, private, or copyrighted content without full retraining. Funded under NSF's Collaborative Research: III: Medium program.
initiatives.significance: Places Wayne State at the forefront of the trust-and-safety research frontier, addressing a core bottleneck for responsible deployment of large models.

initiatives.title: NSF CAREER awards for AI safety and autonomous-vehicle reliability
initiatives.description: Wayne State received multiple NSF CAREER awards in 2025 for AI-focused research. Dr. Zheng Dong received a CAREER award for ChronosDrive, ensuring timing correctness in DNN-driven autonomous vehicles; Dr. Mahbub Islam received a CAREER award in AI reliability.
initiatives.significance: Multiple CAREER awards in a single year indicate a pipeline of federally-recognized junior AI faculty at Wayne State.

initiatives.title: HPC Grid — campus research computing including GPU resources
initiatives.description: Wayne State's Computing & Information Technology operates the Wayne State Grid, a centrally-managed HPC cluster serving research with scalable, GPU-enabled nodes. The Grid is connected via Science DMZ, Internet2, and the Michigan LambdaRail. HPC staff provide consulting and support for grants, and researchers can purchase dedicated GPU hardware hosted by HPC.
initiatives.significance: Campus HPC anchors local AI research, supplemented by Google AI Hypercomputer access via the Google Public Sector partnership.

initiatives.title: AI-Innovations Initiative — School of Medicine / Neurology
initiatives.description: The Department of Neurology within the Wayne State School of Medicine hosts an AI & Innovations Initiative to advance AI in clinical neurology practice, translational research, and medical education.
initiatives.significance: School-of-Medicine-level AI unit expanding beyond engineering and signaling AI's spread across Wayne State's clinical programs.

initiatives.title: AI and Information Literacy module — Wayne State Library System
initiatives.description: The Wayne State Library System publishes an AI and Information Literacy self-study module (1-2 hours; quizzes and activities) and a Using AI Carefully and Thoughtfully guide. The module was adapted from CC-BY licensed materials originally developed by University of Maryland Libraries and the Teaching and Learning Transformation Center.
initiatives.significance: Scalable AI literacy touchpoint accessible outside degree programs; aligns with the 2025-26 Year of Focus and broadens reach beyond engineering.

partners: UMD Teaching and Learning Transformation Center

regulatory_context.summary: Michigan has issued a state government 'AI with Purpose' strategy and established an AI Core Team, though Governor Whitmer was cited by Michigan Advance in late 2025 as earning an 'AI incomplete' for lack of comprehensive higher-ed AI legislation. Michigan has enacted data-center tax exemption legislation to attract large AI infrastructure investments, and federal responsibilities devolve through the Michigan Department of Education. Wayne State is not part of a university system; it is its own public institution with an independent Board of Governors. The federal executive order on AI directs the U.S. Department of Education to provide guidance for education-sector AI use.

controversies_and_litigation.summary: President Kimberly Andrews Espy resigned effective September 17, 2025 after friction with the Board of Governors; Richard Bierschbach named interim president. Provost Laurie Lauzon Clabo then stepped down in November 2025 (continuing as senior adviser through May 2026). The instability coincided with AI-strategy launches (Grand Challenges, AIDaS, Year of Focus) and may affect sustained AI-strategy execution.
Provost Laurie Lauzon Clabo stepped down, the second major leadership departure in two months, though she remains as senior adviser to the interim president through the academic year

narrative_summary: Wayne State University — Michigan's only urban R1 research university, serving 24,168 students in Detroit — has moved decisively in 2025 to institutionalize its AI posture under a new Grand Challenges research framework where AI sits as one of four pillars alongside health, mobility, and environmental sustainability. The flagship structural move is the Board-approved Institute for AI and Data Science (AIDaS), chartered October 23, 2025 for six years under the Vice President for Research & Innovation and co-directed by computer scientist Dr. Dongxiao Zhu and mathematician Dr. Hengguang Li. AIDaS consolidates the Wayne AI Research Initiative, Data Science and Business Analytics, and Computation/AI/Data Science groups into one interdisciplinary hub spanning engineering, medicine, business, and the liberal arts. Wayne State's strategy is paired with a cautious but comprehensive AI-use policy through Computing & Information Technology: a whitelist of approved tools (WarriorGPT, Microsoft Copilot, Google Vertex AI, Cursor AI, Zoom AI Companion, with ChatGPT and Claude requiring exception approval) and explicit prohibitions on FERPA/PHI/PII inputs. The Board of Governors amended the Student Code of Conduct in 2025 at the Academic Senate AI Committee's recommendation to require students to follow instructor-level AI policy, preserving faculty discretion while codifying accountability. On the deployment side, Wayne State runs WarriorGPT as its FERPA-aware campus GPT, the long-running W the Warrior SMS chatbot (2018; cited for 14.6% enrollment lift and strong first-gen/Pell impact), a June 2025 Google Public Sector partnership powering the PHOENIX public-health platform and Syntasa-backed CHNA 2.0 tool, and the January 2026 Kyndryl IntelliMake pilot-scale AI manufacturing factory in the engineering college. Academic offerings include a 30-credit M.S. in AI with three concentrations (Algorithms, Hardware, Industrial), an online AI certificate, and a new 'Trustworthy AI for LLMs and Vision-Language Models' course in Winter 2026. Federal research funding spans NSF IUCRC ($4.5M eCAT center), NIH biomedical AI grants ($4M+), and multiple NSF CAREER awards. The 2025-26 Year of Focus 'AI in Action' organizes campus-wide programming including a Sal Khan keynote. Significant caveats: President Espy resigned in September 2025 and Provost Clabo departed in November 2025, creating executive-level uncertainty during the critical institutionalization phase of Wayne State's AI strategy. There is no Chief AI Officer role, and the AI policy adoption date is not publicly documented.
